questions.prompt: A user types example.com into their browser. Which step happens last?
choices: The recursive resolver asks the .com TLD nameservers who is authoritative for example.com
The recursive resolver asks the authoritative nameservers for the IP address
The browser opens a TCP connection to the IP address that came back
The user's device sends the question to its configured recursive resolver
questions.explanation: DNS only resolves the name to an IP. The browser opens the TCP connection only after the IP is returned. This is why a domain can resolve perfectly while the website is still down (the IP is reachable to DNS but not to your TCP connection).

questions.prompt: When you change a DNS record, every resolver on the internet sees the new value within seconds.
questions.explanation: Resolvers cache answers for the TTL of the record. Until each resolver's cache expires, it serves the old value. 'DNS propagation' is really 'caches expiring at different rates'.
